name: Kusaal
notes: Kusaal (Kʋsaal) — Mabia/Gur (Atlantic-Congo) language of northeastern Ghana and adjacent Burkina Faso and Togo. Written in a Latin-based alphabet using Africa Alphabet letters (ɛ, ɔ, ʋ, ŋ). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: Kusaal is tonal and has an ATR vowel-harmony system; a reliable spec must model the vowel set, tone and the two written dialects (Agole and Toende), which requires dedicated sourcing. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Latin-script Kusaal orthography. wikidata_qid left unset: the code's Wikidata row resolves to the Wikimedia project item, not the language item; not recorded rather than record a wrong id.
